bills.title: A joint resolution providing for congressional disapproval under chapter 8 of title 5, United States Code, of the rule submitted by the Bureau of Industry and Security of the Department of Commerce relating to "One Year Suspension of Expansion of End-User Controls for Affiliates of Certain Listed Entities".
bills.latest_action: Placed on Senate Legislative Calendar under General Orders. Calendar No. 403.

bills.title: A resolution recognizing escalating threats to freedom of the press and freedom of speech worldwide, including increasing harm to journalists reporting in conflict zones and under repressive regimes, reaffirming the vital role that a free and independent press plays in upholding democracy, fostering economic prosperity, and keeping the public informed, and reaffirming freedom of the press as a priority of the United States Government in supporting democracy, human rights, and good governance in commemoration of "World Press Freedom Day" on May 3, 2026.
bills.latest_action: Referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations. (text: CR S2177-2178)

bills.title: A resolution celebrating the 154th anniversary of Arbor Day.
bills.latest_action: Submitted in the Senate, considered, and agreed to without amendment and with a preamble by Unanimous Consent. (consideration: CR S2160; text: CR S2180)

bills.title: A resolution amending rule XXXVII of the Standing Rules of the Senate to prohibit Senators from trading on prediction markets.
bills.latest_action: Resolution agreed to in Senate with amendments by Unanimous Consent.

bills.title: A bill to amend the FISA Amendments Act of 2008 to extend the authorities of title VII of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978, and for other purposes.
bills.latest_action: Became Public Law No: 119-87.

bills.title: A resolution expressing support for the designation of April 1, 2026, through April 30, 2026, as "Fair Chance Jobs Month".
bills.latest_action: Referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions. (text: CR S2134)

bills.title: Calling on elected officials and civil society leaders to counter antisemitism and educate the public on the contributions of the Jewish-American community.
